title: corrigiola litoralis subsp. telephiifolia
intro: Corrigiola litoralis subsp. telephiifolia, also known as Sea Pinks, is a succulent subshrub that belongs to the Madder family and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of anthraquinones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Corrigiola litoralis subsp. telephiifolia include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used as a natural dye in traditional medicine.
